D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

JPEG 4:2:0 Chroma Subsampling — Save Bits

Convert RGB → YCbCr, then store Y at full resolution but Cb, Cr at 1/2 in each dimension; works because eye is more sensitive to luminance than chrominance

4:4:4 = full chroma (no subsampling), 4:2:2 = halved horizontally, 4:2:0 = halved both ways (standard JPEG). 4:2:0 stores 50 % fewer chroma samples — saving ~1/3 total bits with little visible damage on natural images. Fails on red text on grey background and saturated thin lines.

Toggle subsampling scheme.
